definition:
1. properly, cooked meat , or, generally, meat , opposite to bread and other provisions, (Homer) 
2. anything eaten with bread  or food , to give it flavour and relish, κρόμυον, ποτῷ ὄψον onions, a zest  or relish  to wine, (Iliad by Homer); ἐσθίουσι ἐπὶ τῷ σίτῳ ὄψον (Xenophon Historicus) 
3. seasoning, sauce , (Plato Philosophus); κολλύραν καὶ κόνδυλον ὄψον ἐπ᾽ αὐτῇ pudding and knuckle- sauce  withal, (Aristophanes Comicus); λιμῷ ὅσαπερ ὄψῳ διαχρῆσθε, i. e. "hunger is the best sauce," (Xenophon Historicus) 
4. generally, dainty fare , in plural dainties , (Plato Philosophus) 
5. at Athens, mostly, fish , the chief dainty of the Athenians, (Aristophanes Comicus) 
6. the fish-market , (Aristophanes Comicus) (ML)
